task_completion.description: Create at least two distinct simulated device adapters in the OpenICE Supervisor
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the OpenICE Supervisor GUI with at least two different simulated devices (e.g., Multiparameter Monitor, Pulse Oximeter, Capnograph) actively running or configured.

task_completion.description: Confirm data flow through the Vital Signs clinical application
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the OpenICE Vital Signs application GUI open and displaying active data (such as waveforms, numeric readings, or charts) from the simulated devices.

task_completion.description: Analyze runtime logs or source code to identify DDS middleware topics and ICE data models
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a terminal, file manager, or text editor displaying the contents of '/home/ga/openice/logs/openice.log' or files within the '/opt/openice/mdpnp/' directory (like ice.idl).
Screenshots showing a text editor saving the file as 'ehr_integration_report.txt' on the Desktop, or a file manager/terminal showing the file exists at '/home/ga/Desktop/ehr_integration_report.txt'.

task_completion.description: Include the four required sections in the feasibility report
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the report text clearly showing the exact headings: 'Device Data Inventory', 'Data Architecture', 'Integration Recommendations', and 'Observed Data Characteristics'.

task_completion.description: Document technically accurate findings regarding DDS middleware, ICE data models, and EHR integration
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the report text containing technical keywords such as DDS, topic, publish, subscribe, RTPS, ice.Numeric, ice.SampleArray, HL7, FHIR, or Mirth.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual OpenICE GUI applications
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual windows of the OpenICE Supervisor and Vital Signs applications being opened and manipulated, rather than the agent just guessing or writing the report without running the software.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely inspected the logs or source code
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for commands like 'cat', 'less', 'grep', or a text editor opening the specific log file or mdpnp source files, proving the agent didn't just hallucinate the architecture details.

integrity.description: Agent wrote a genuine feasibility report rather than copying the prompt
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in 'ehr_integration_report.txt' should contain full sentences and actual technical analysis, not just a copy-paste of the task description or a bare list of the required section headers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The report must be saved at '/home/ga/Desktop/ehr_integration_report.txt' and be at least 500 bytes. It must include exactly these four section headers: 'Device Data Inventory', 'Data Architecture', 'Integration Recommendations', and 'Observed Data Characteristics'. The OpenICE architecture uses OMG DDS middleware (topics, domains, participants, publish-subscribe, RTPS protocol) and IDL for data models. Real ICE data models include ice.Numeric (slow rate <=3Hz), ice.SampleArray (high rate >3Hz), ice.InfusionStatus, and ice.AlarmLimit. Integration keywords to look for include HL7, FHIR, Mirth, interface, gateway, adapter, and API. Simulated devices include Multiparameter Monitor, Pulse Oximeter, Capnograph, Infusion Pump, Electrocardiogram, and SpO2.
